title: Cut-Fill Balance Optimizer
description: Industrial-grade earthwork balancing tool for mass haul optimization, minimizing rehandle and haul costs using lean construction and Six Sigma principles.

inputs.label: Cut Volume
inputs.businessContext: Total volume of material to be excavated from cut areas, measured in bank cubic meters (BCM).

businessContext_i18n.en: Total volume of material to be excavated from cut areas, measured in bank cubic meters (BCM).

inputs.label: Fill Volume
inputs.businessContext: Total volume of material required for fill/embankment areas, measured in compacted cubic meters (CCM).

businessContext_i18n.en: Total volume of material required for fill/embankment areas, measured in compacted cubic meters (CCM).

inputs.label: Swell Factor
inputs.businessContext: Factor accounting for volume increase when material is excavated (loose state vs. bank state). Typical range 1.1–1.4.

businessContext_i18n.en: Factor accounting for volume increase when material is excavated (loose state vs. bank state). Typical range 1.1–1.4.

inputs.label: Shrinkage Factor
inputs.businessContext: Factor accounting for volume decrease when material is compacted (compacted state vs. bank state). Typical range 0.75–0.95.

businessContext_i18n.en: Factor accounting for volume decrease when material is compacted (compacted state vs. bank state). Typical range 0.75–0.95.

inputs.label: Average Haul Distance
inputs.businessContext: Average one-way distance from cut to fill areas, used for cost and fuel calculations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average one-way distance from cut to fill areas, used for cost and fuel calculations.

inputs.label: Unit Haul Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost per cubic meter per kilometer of hauling material, including fuel, labor, and equipment depreciation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost per cubic meter per kilometer of hauling material, including fuel, labor, and equipment depreciation.

inputs.label: Waste Disposal Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost per cubic meter for disposing excess material at a designated waste site.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost per cubic meter for disposing excess material at a designated waste site.

inputs.label: Borrow Material Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost per cubic meter to import material from a borrow pit to meet fill deficit.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost per cubic meter to import material from a borrow pit to meet fill deficit.

rules.message: Both cut and fill volumes must be greater than zero for a valid balance calculation.
Swell factor must be >= 1.0 and shrinkage factor <= 1.0 to reflect physical reality.
Unit haul cost must be positive.

thresholds.message: Cut volume exceeds fill volume by more than 30%. Consider waste disposal or design adjustment.
Fill volume exceeds cut volume by more than 30%. Consider borrow material or design adjustment.
Haul cost per cubic meter exceeds $5.00. Investigate alternative routes or equipment.
Cut-fill imbalance exceeds 50%. Major rehandle or import/export expected.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
